## prompt-4-verify-passage

*Generated by Process A from the submitted `prompts/prompt-enactment.md`. This stage
is new to the v0.3.0 build and has no direct analogue in
`adoption/sub-prompts/`; it is inserted specifically between the full and final
source files to double verify that every table and every figure is correct before
the manuscript is carried to publication quality. It does not rewrite the prior
adoption framework.*

Write the verify-framework into `law-physical-ai-trials/enactment/verify-framework/`.
This stage does not add new prose. It reads the full-framework source critically,
runs a complete table-and-figure audit against the instructions below, records the
findings in a verification report, and emits the corrected source files (and an
Overleaf bundle) that the final stage will polish. Every defect found is fixed here
and certified; nothing ambiguous is passed forward.

**Table audit (perform for every table).**

- Width: every table is set to exactly `\textwidth` and matches the body measure.
- Columns: every column is `>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}` (left
  aligned, ragged right), never a bare `p{}` or `l`/`c`/`r` that would leave large
  inter-word gaps. The `Y` (tabularx) column is permitted for the one flexible
  column.
- Column widths optimized for aesthetics: tune each fixed width to the amount of
  text the column actually carries, so the narrow columns are narrow and the prose
  column is wide, and so no cell wraps to a lonely one word line. Confirm the widths
  plus inter-column space sum to `\textwidth`.
- Guard: every table is preceded by a `\needspace` large enough that the table is
  not split across a page break, and is referenced with `\autoref`.

**Figure audit (perform twice for every figure).** Read each `psgfig` block, reason
about the geometry, fix any defect, then read it a second time to confirm the fix:

a. No text box and arrow overlaps: confirm no connector passes through a node;
   reroute edges to specific anchors (`.north`, `.south`, `.east`, `.west`, and the
   corner anchors) and add bends where a straight edge would cross a box; confirm no
   label sits on top of a box.
b. Curved arrows always carry the correct specified looseness: every curved edge
   written as `to[out=...,in=...]` must include an explicit `looseness=<value>`, and
   the value must be appropriate to the bend (a gentle return uses a small
   looseness near 0.4 to 0.6; a tight loop uses a larger value); confirm none is
   missing or left at the default.
c. Proper spacings between boxes: confirm node coordinates are evenly spaced (a
   consistent horizontal pitch and vertical pitch), no two boxes touch or overlap,
   and the figure fits within the body measure with the `\fcolorbox` frame.

**Caption rule.** Confirm a uniform `\vspace{-0.75cm}` sits between every figure and
its caption, with no other ad hoc spacing between the two.

**Palette check.** Confirm every figure uses only black, grayscales, `#4B0082`,
`#000080`, and `#C0C0C0`, and that the template accent is `#4B0082`. Flag and
correct any stray hue.

**Output of this stage.** A `verification-report.md` that lists, table by table and
figure by figure, what was checked and what was changed (with a pass or fix verdict
for each of the table tests and each of the three figure tests, twice for figures);
the corrected `main.tex`, `passagestyle.sty`, `references.bib`, and `sections/*.tex`;
a `verify-framework-LaTeX.zip` Overleaf bundle; and a `README.md`.

One commit each for `main.tex`, the `.sty`, `references.bib`, and `README.md`; one
commit per `sections/*.tex` file; one commit for `verification-report.md`; plus
`prompt-verify-passage.md` and `output-verify-passage.md`. For the second-to-last
commit fix all remaining errors; for the last commit write the directory README and
the Overleaf bundle and refresh the pull request. The corrected manuscript must
compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX. Commit and push in real time; do not hold
commits. Do not stall, ask questions, or go into plan mode.
